parts.title: Part 1
parts.description: For each question, choose the correct answer.

questions.content: COLLEGE SHOP: Special offers on all study materials this week. Open 12:30-13:30 daily except Monday.

options.text: Students must go to the college shop to buy certain items for their studies.
Students can take advantage of special offers at the college shop at the moment.
Students may visit the college shop at lunchtime every day except Monday.

questions.explanation: 'Special offers this week' matches 'take advantage of special offers at the moment'.

questions.content: Hi Max, my cousin can't come shopping tomorrow as planned. Let's do it another time when he's around. Jake

options.text: suggest a new date for a shopping trip.
invite Max to meet one of his relatives.
cancel an arrangement he had made.

questions.explanation: Jake is canceling the shopping trip planned for tomorrow because his cousin can't come.

questions.content: PIZZA PLANET: Buy any two pizzas before 7 p.m. and get the cheaper one free! (Excludes delivery)

options.text: All the pizzas in the restaurant are included in this offer.
Any pizza ordered before 7 p.m. costs less than the usual price.
At certain times, a customer can get two pizzas for the price of one.

questions.explanation: 'Buy two... get cheaper one free' before 7 p.m. means two for the price of one during specific times.

questions.content: To all students: I have placed a box outside my office for any suggestions you have on how to improve waste recycling in the school. Mr Wood.

options.text: Mr Wood has made suggestions on how to recycle waste produced by the school.
Recycling materials should be put into the box outside Mr Wood's office.
Mr Wood wants new ideas on ways to improve waste recycling in the school.

questions.explanation: He is asking for 'any suggestions' which means he wants 'new ideas'.

questions.content: Thanks for the cinema invite, Sam. That horror film was terrifying, though! Let's watch something less scary together next time. Mel

options.text: accept Sam's invitation to the cinema.
admit to Sam that she found the film scary.
ask when Sam can see another film with her.

questions.explanation: She explicitely says 'That horror film was terrifying'.

parts.title: Part 2
parts.description: Decide which book would be the most suitable for each person.
parts.passage:
Eight Book Reviews:
A. Being Me: Practical advice for health. Recipes from athletes and exercise routines.
B. Endless Days: True story of first pilots flying across Atlantic. Importance for modern world.
C. Action Plan: Interviews with famous athletes about their careers and preparation.
D. Memories: Journey around the world, cultures and beautiful nature.
E. Days in the Sun: Irish farm life and how travel inspired the author's style.
F. Parrots in Paradise: Adventure story of a girl lost on an island with birds.
G. Night Light: Autobiography of a blind world-class athlete and his challenges.
H. Our Lives: Traditional meals and life in different parts of the world. Photos of wildlife.

questions.text: Ahmed: Enjoys reading about real people telling their own story and dealing with difficulties.

options.text: Night Light: Autobiography of a blind athlete and his challenges.

questions.explanation: Ahmed wants real people (autobiography) and difficulties (blind athlete's challenges).

questions.text: Pritti: Loves sport. Wants to learn about top sportspeople and how they achieve goals.

options.text: Action Plan: Interviews with famous athletes about their careers and preparation.

questions.explanation: Matches her interest in athletes' careers and how they succeed.

questions.text: Coral: Loves nature and writing. Wants to develop writing skills for a career as a writer.

options.text: Days in the Sun: Irish farm life and how the author improved his writing style.

questions.explanation: The author describes how he improved his style, which helps someone wanting to be a writer.

questions.text: Elif: Likes adventures and brave people in the past who influenced today's actions.

options.text: Endless Days: True story of first pilots and their importance for the modern world.

questions.explanation: First pilots are brave people from the past, and it mentions 'importance for modern world'.

questions.text: Stefan: Enjoys traveling, cultures, and learning typical dishes (recipes).

options.text: Our Lives: Traditional meals from different parts of the world and cultures.

questions.explanation: Covers cultures and recipes ('traditional meals') from different parts of the world.

parts.title: Part 3
parts.description: Read the story of Vicky, a cyclist, and choose the correct answer.
parts.passage:
Vicky's Cycling Career

I started cycling because of my older brother. He had this amazing professional bike and he used to let me try it in the park. One day, he just said 'It's yours,' and that was the beginning of everything. I didn't have a coach or fancy advice from my parents, it was just me and that bike.

After winning multiple national championships, people were surprised when I announced my retirement at 25. They thought I was too young, but honestly, I'd achieved everything I ever dreamed of. I wanted to start a new chapter while I still had the energy for other things.

Now I work with CycleZone, a charity for young people. We don't just focus on racing; our main goal is to help kids find their inner strength and become more confident in themselves through cycling.

options.text: she had a very good coach.
her parents gave her helpful advice.
she could get to a race track easily.
her brother gave her a great bike.

questions.explanation: She says 'He [brother] had this amazing professional bike... one day he said It's yours'.

questions.text: Why does Vicky say she stopped cycle racing?

options.text: She felt she was too old to do it.
She was becoming bored with it.
She had won everything she wanted.
She was preparing for a new career.

questions.explanation: She states 'I'd achieved everything I ever dreamed of'.

questions.text: What does the charity CycleZone do for young people?

options.text: It teaches them how to do track racing.
It supports those who have talent.
It offers them the chance to try a variety of sports.
It helps them become more confident.

questions.explanation: The main goal is to 'help kids... become more confident in themselves'.

parts.title: Part 4
parts.description: Choose which sentence fits each gap in the text.
parts.passage:
At home together

Rudolf House is a care home in Helsinki. Some rooms are not easily accessible for the elderly, so the city council decided to rent them to young people. They called it 'The House that Fits'.

When Taimi (83) heard about the plan, she wondered how it was going to work. (16) _____ Then, a young man named Jonatan appeared. (17) _____ 'Hi! I'm your new neighbor,' he said. (18) _____ Surprising herself, Taimi started talking about her life. Jonatan was training to become a chef. (19) _____ That's when he applied to live at Rudolf House. In the end, three young people were chosen. (20) _____ That's how their friendship began.

options.text: It would be the first time that anything like this had happened at Rudolf House.

questions.explanation: Explains why she was wondering how it would work.

options.text: She'd left the door open, as she always did in the morning.

questions.explanation: Explains how the young man could just appear outside her room.

options.text: Instead, they just talked, as if they'd known each other forever.

questions.explanation: Fits the context of them starting a conversation immediately.

options.text: Therefore, he needed to find somewhere to live.

questions.explanation: Because he was training in the city but his family moved away.

options.text: In the end, three of them, including Jonatan, were chosen.

questions.explanation: Summarizes the selection process for 'The House that Fits'.

parts.title: Part 5
parts.description: Choose the correct word to fill in the gaps.
parts.passage:
The invention of crisps

Potato crisps were invented by accident in 1853, by a chef called George Crum. He was extremely (21) _____ of his cookery skills. One evening, a customer complained about the potatoes being too thick. Crum cut them paper-thin and fried them until hard. He (22) _____ them to taste disgusting, but the customer loved them! News travelled fast and a (23) _____ global industry grew from this accident.

options.text: proud
satisfied
pleased
impressed

questions.explanation: You are 'proud of' something.

options.text: hoped
intended
attempted
tried

questions.explanation: He 'intended' them to be bad, but they were good.

options.text: big
huge
large
wide

questions.explanation: 'Huge' is a common adjective for a massive global industry.

parts.title: Part 6
parts.description: Write one word only to fill each gap.
parts.passage:
Moving house

Hi Alex, I finally moved house! (24) _____ wasn't until I started to pack that I realised how much stuff I had. I think moving house is one of (25) _____ most stressful things I've ever done! I'm looking (26) _____ to being in this new house. It's got a great garden, (27) _____ least. I'm having a party on Saturday night, so why don't you come along (28) _____ you are free?

questions.explanation: Dummy subject 'It wasn't until...'
Superlative 'the most stressful'.
Phrasal verb 'looking forward to'.
Idiom 'at least'.
Conditional 'if you are free'.
